behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure commonly appears at the boundary between HTTP raw-body capture and signature verification, not just inside one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; an in-process HTTP app test is enough. REJECTION-GATE: I rejected System because the behavior text only requires 'rejected with 401' and 'not forwarded' (no real network condition or OS primitive).
behaviors.test_description: Start the Node handler in-process with real middleware wiring (raw body parser + signature verifier + queue adapter test double). Send a request with mismatched `X-Signature-256` over the in-process HTTP boundary and assert `401` plus zero enqueue calls.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is an ordering guarantee across handler and queue persistence modules. Unit is too low because the bug is in handoff/order between modules. REAL-INFRA: local persistence backend (e.g., local DB/file queue) is sufficient; no external network/OS-only behavior is required. REJECTION-GATE: I rejected System because behavior text specifies sequencing ('written ... before ... 200') but not real network/subprocess constraints.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with the real queue implementation against a local test persistence store. Delay queue write completion deterministically and assert HTTP 200 is not emitted until write commit succeeds; then assert committed queue record exists.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is about interaction between request handler, queue write, and async processor trigger. A single-function unit test can miss real await/Promise wiring bugs. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network required. REJECTION-GATE: I rejected System because behavior text is about not waiting for downstream completion, not about real network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream worker module in-process. Make downstream processing intentionally slow/blocking while queue write succeeds quickly. Assert response returns immediately after enqueue/persist step and before downstream completion signal.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is an explicit wall-clock SLA under concurrency; real HTTP server scheduling/timers/concurrency behavior can hide failures that in-process fakes miss. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timing or fake transport can pass while real event-loop/socket contention violates 3s. REJECTION-GATE: I rejected Integration because behavior text requires 'within 3 seconds wall-clock' under 'slow and concurrent' load, which is a real runtime/system boundary concern.
behaviors.test_description: Launch the service process, generate concurrent real HTTP webhook traffic where earlier events trigger slow downstream work, and measure end-to-end response latency for new requests. Fail if p100 (or strict per-request assertion) exceeds 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedupe correctness is at module/state boundary (handler -> queue/store with existing IDs). Unit logic alone can miss persistence/lookup race or handoff bugs. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue is enough; no need for external system boundary. REJECTION-GATE: I rejected System because behavior text focuses on dedupe write semantics, not real network/OS primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with real dedupe storage path. Send two requests with same event ID (including near-concurrent submission). Assert only one persisted queue entry and one accepted enqueue effect.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text says 'queue reports it is full'—this is handler-to-queue error mapping across module boundary. Unit may miss integration wiring (error type translation/header emission). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency is required for this behavior itself. REJECTION-GATE: I rejected System because this behavior does not require validating real capacity mechanics, only HTTP mapping when full is reported.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP handler wired to queue module/test double that returns a canonical 'full' error. Assert HTTP 503 and correct `Retry-After` header value; assert no success enqueue path executed.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: mocked queue always passing would hide real capacity-enforcement bugs; must exercise real persistent queue implementation with its storage layer. REAL-INFRA: this can be validated with local DB/filesystem integration (fits Integration definition). REJECTION-GATE: I rejected System because behavior text requires real queue implementation semantics, but not external HTTP/network/subprocess conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against a local test store with small configured capacity. Insert events to the limit, then one more; assert explicit rejection and unchanged persisted depth beyond capacity.
